title: General Microbiology
catalogDescription: Acquaints students with microorganisms and their activities. Topics include microbial cell structure and function, metabolism, microbial genetics, and the role of microorganisms in disease, immunity, and other selected applied areas. Course overlaps with: B HLTH 228; B HLTH 402; and T BIOL 331. Prerequisite: either CHEM 120, CHEM 142, or CHEM 145. Offered: ASp.
prerequisiteNotes: Official UW prerequisite text: either CHEM 120, CHEM 142, or CHEM 145.

title: General Microbiology Laboratory
catalogDescription: Laboratory course primarily for students taking MICROM 301. Covers a variety of microbiological techniques, with experiments designed to illustrate major concepts of bacteriology, virology, and immunology. No auditors. Offered: ASp.

title: Fundamentals of General Microbiology Laboratory
catalogDescription: Isolation of a broad range of nonpathogenic bacteria from natural sources, using selective and enrichment techniques, with microscopic, biochemical, and molecular identification. Related exercises include genetics, physiology, quantitation, and growth energetics. Prerequisite: BIOL 200. Offered: ASp.

title: Fundamentals of General Microbiology I
catalogDescription: Survey of the microbial world, metabolism, biosynthesis, regulation, growth, structure, and function. Required for students majoring in microbiology; recommended for students majoring in biology. Course overlaps with: T BIOL 331. Prerequisite: a minimum grade of 2.3 in BIOL 200; and either CHEM 223, CHEM 237, or CHEM 257. Offered: A.

title: Bacteria and Phage Genetics
catalogDescription: Reviews molecular genetics, including description of fundamental genetic processes such as mutation, repair, genetic exchange, recombination, and gene expression. Use of genetic strategies to analyze complex biological processes. Focuses on bacteria and bacteriophages. Prerequisite: BIOL 200; and either CHEM 223, CHEM 237, or CHEM 257; recommended: either MICROM 301, MICROM 402, or MICROM 410. Offered: W.
prerequisiteNotes: Official UW prerequisite text: BIOL 200; and either CHEM 223, CHEM 237, or CHEM 257;
Official UW recommended-preparation text: either MICROM 301, MICROM 402, or MICROM 410.

title: Prokaryotic Diversity
catalogDescription: Structure, biochemical properties, and genetics of the major groups of prokaryotes. Prerequisite: BIOL 200. Offered: Sp.

title: Prokaryotic Recombinant DNA Techniques
catalogDescription: Laboratory course emphasizing concepts and techniques/methodologies in recombinant DNA research employing bacteria and their viruses. Topics and experiments/demonstrations include genomic and plasmid DNA isolation, restriction mapping, cloning, transposon mutagenesis, sequencing, and Western and Southern blotting. No auditors. Prerequisite: either BIOL 200 or MICROM 301. Offered: Sp.
prerequisiteNotes: Official UW prerequisite text: either BIOL 200 or MICROM 301.

title: Medical Virology
catalogDescription: Introduces medical virology emphasizing basic understanding of the pathogenesis and replication of medically important viruses including the spread, diagnosis, epidemiology, treatment, and prevention of human viral diseases. Prerequisite: either BIOL 180 or BIOL 200.
